text.title: Launch Retro Output Contract
text.summary: Pins the shape of the launch-retro doc. Subagent must satisfy this before committing the next-launch change.

field.name: per_section_data
field.constraint: every section opens with numbers, not narrative

field.name: keep_change_drop
field.constraint: each list has ≥ 1 entry across the full retro

field.name: committed_change
field.constraint: exactly ONE committed change object per retro

field.name: participants
field.constraint: participants >= 2 (single-person retro is not a retro)

forbidden.pattern: Sections collapse engineering-incident discussion into marketing retro (violates r1)
Sections opening with narrative before data (violates r2)
Blame language present: "X failed", "Y dropped the ball" (violates r3)
keep_change_drop entries with vague language ("think about", "consider", "explore") (violates r4)
retro_date older than launch_date + 14 days (memory decayed)

allowed-transformations.transformation: Add a "celebrate" section before the 5 standard ones; celebration is a marker, not a substitute for the rules.
Defer one big lesson to the NEXT retro if the team needs more data; document the defer explicitly.
Cross-link to engineering incident retro for context, but do not absorb its content.

list.item: Five sections covered with marketing focus
Every section opens with data, not narrative
keep / change / drop entries are concrete
Exactly one committed change with 3 named owners
Retro held within 14 days of launch
